edge_hypothesis: The ema_rsi_atr template has been 'stagnant' at PF=0.97-1.03 (advisory status: STAGNANT, deprioritized). But it has NEVER been Claude-specced — all prior results used pipeline-generated default parameters with standard 1.0x ATR stops. This template has a unique feature no other template shares: a built-in ATR > ATR_MA20 volatility expansion gate. This gate means we ONLY enter when current volatility exceeds the 20-period average — i.e., the market is expanding, not contracting. The hypothesis: the volatility expansion gate is actually a VALUABLE filter (it ensures entry at the start of expanding moves), but its value was nullified by (a) default RSI bands too wide to provide meaningful confirmation (40-70 allows almost any RSI), (b) standard 1.0x ATR stops that get hit immediately IN the very volatility expansion the gate detected. By applying the validated wide R:R principle (2.0x stop / 4.0x TP) and tightening RSI bands to 45-60 (requiring RSI to be in the sweet spot of 'trending but not overbought'), we let the volatility expansion develop instead of being stopped out by it. This is the first template rehabilitation attempt — if PF > 1.1, the ema_rsi_atr template was misclassified as dead. Regime assumption: TRENDING (EMA_9 > EMA_21 is explicitly trend-following). Invalidation: if PF < 1.0 with wide R:R and tightened RSI, the template truly has no edge — the signal itself is noise, not just the parameters.

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_volatile_trend_balanced
variants.description: EMA_9/21 trend + RSI 45-60 sweet spot + ATR expansion gate with 2.0x stop / 4.0x TP. The balanced rehabilitation variant. RSI 45-60 is tighter than default (40-70) — requires momentum without overbought exhaustion. The ATR expansion gate naturally filters low-volatility chop. Wide R:R (2:1) proven across MACD/supertrend/RSI pullback. This tests whether ema_rsi_atr was 'stagnant' due to bad parameters or bad signal.
entry_long: RSI_14 between 45 and 60 — trending but not overbought.
entry_short: RSI_14 between 40 and 55 — trending down but not oversold.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Volatility gate: ATR_14 must exceed its own 20-period MA (built into template)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (4.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if trade count < 20, RSI band is too tight — widen to 42-65
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 4.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9/21 trend direction — short-term trend confirmation
RSI_14 sweet spot (45-60 long / 40-55 short) — tighter than default for quality
ATR_14 dual role: volatility expansion gate (> MA20) + 2.0x stop / 4.0x TP sizing

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_volatile_trend_wide_rr
variants.description: EMA_9/21 trend + RSI 42-65 (wider band for more trades) + ATR expansion gate with 2.0x stop / 5.0x TP (2.5:1 R:R). Wider RSI acceptance range than balanced variant to maximize trade count. The wider R:R (2.5:1) gives winners more room to run in volatile conditions. This variant tests whether more permissive RSI + extreme patience (5.0x TP) extracts more from the volatility expansion gate.
entry_long: RSI_14 between 42 and 65 — broad trending zone.
entry_short: RSI_14 between 35 and 58 — broad trending zone for shorts.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Volatility gate: ATR_14 must exceed its own 20-period MA (built into template)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (5.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 with wider RSI, the narrower band from balanced variant was needed
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9/21 trend direction — short-term trend confirmation
RSI_14 wide band (42-65 long / 35-58 short) — maximizes trade count
ATR_14 dual role: expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 5.0x TP — wider R:R for vol expansion

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_volatile_trend_tight_quality
variants.description: EMA_9/21 trend + RSI 48-58 (tight quality band) + ATR expansion gate with 1.5x stop / 3.5x TP (2.3:1 R:R matching the MACD ACCEPT's proven ratio). The quality variant — RSI 48-58 for longs means we only enter when RSI shows strong but not extreme momentum. This will produce fewer trades but higher quality. Uses the ACCEPT's proven 2.3:1 R:R ratio. Tests whether extreme RSI tightening + ACCEPT R:R rescues the template.
entry_long: RSI_14 between 48 and 58 — strong momentum, not overbought.
entry_short: RSI_14 between 42 and 52 — strong downward momentum, not oversold.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Volatility gate: ATR_14 must exceed its own 20-period MA (built into template)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (3.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if trade count < 15, RSI 48-58 is too restrictive for 4h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 3.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9/21 trend direction — short-term trend confirmation
RSI_14 tight band (48-58 long / 42-52 short) — maximum signal quality
ATR_14 dual role: expansion gate + 1.5x stop / 3.5x TP — ACCEPT R:R ratio
